preview_text:
The model rates Uzbekistan as the narrow favorite with a 47% win probability, while the model assigns DR Congo a 23% chance of victory. The gap between the two win probabilities reflects a meaningful but not decisive advantage in the forecast. A draw is the second-likeliest outcome at 30%, suggesting neither side is expected to pull away decisively.

The 30% draw probability indicates a competitive match where a stalemate remains plausible. Uzbekistan's win odds exceed DR Congo's by roughly 24 percentage points in the model's view, but the substantial draw chance means all three outcomes carry material weight. The distribution suggests a contested fixture without a clear runaway favorite.

The market consensus diverges notably from the model. Sportsbooks favor DR Congo at 41% versus the model's 23%, a gap of roughly 18 percentage points. Simultaneously, books rate Uzbekistan at 30% compared to the model's 47%, a 17-point spread in the opposite direction. Polymarket prices align closely with sportsbooks. This dashboard offers the model's view but does not adjudicate which forecast proves more reliable.
